engineIntelligence.h2: Subaru R2 Engines - Codes, Common Failures & Replacement Costs
engineIntelligence.description: The cards below cover the main engines fitted to the Subaru R2 in the UK - including documented failure causes, variant production years, and typical rebuilt engine costs based on current UK market data. All R2 engines use the EN07 inline-4 architecture, not Subaru's boxer design.

cards.altText: Subaru R2 EN07E engine - petrol 0.7L replacement UK
knownFailures: Oil consumption - piston ring wear causes blue exhaust smoke on higher-mileage units.
Timing belt failure - interference engine design means snapped belt bends valves catastrophically.

cards.altText: Subaru R2 EN07D engine - petrol 0.7L replacement UK
knownFailures: AVCS solenoid failure - variable valve timing system can cause rough idle and power loss.
Valve clearance noise - SOHC/DOHC tappet adjustment required at service intervals.

cards.altText: Subaru R2 EN07X engine - petrol 0.7L replacement UK
knownFailures: Supercharger failure - IHI supercharger bearing wear causes boost loss and whining noise.
Intercooler boost leaks - hose degradation reduces performance on high-mileage STi models.
cards.replacementCost: Typical UK rebuilt engine: £1,495-£3,250 (supply only) Estimated from comparable EN07 units - actual quotes may vary.

commonProblems.h2: Subaru R2 Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Subaru R2's EN07 inline-4 engines are generally reliable but suffer from oil consumption on higher-mileage units and timing belt risks due to interference design. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded piston rings and new timing components - plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty.

problems.h4: Oil Consumption on EN07 Engines - Why R2 Engines Burn Oil
problems.affectedModels: Subaru R2 R, S, Classic, STi (2003-2010, EN07E/EN07D/EN07X)
problems.rootCause: Piston ring wear and valve stem seal degradation allow oil to enter combustion chambers, causing blue exhaust smoke and frequent top-ups.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace valve stem seals via top-end service, no engine removal
repairOptions.longevity: Temporary fix, may reduce smoke but ring wear remains

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove cylinder head, replace piston rings and seals, reassemble
repairOptions.longevity: Addresses root cause but labour-intensive on tight kei engine bay

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt EN07 with upgraded rings, new timing belt, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best long-term value, eliminates oil consumption and includes warranty

problems.recommendation: On a Subaru R2 worth £1,500-£4,000, a rebuilt EN07 engine with upgraded rings offers better value than repeated top-end repairs. Our specialists include oil consumption fixes as standard on all rebuilt units.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt EN07 engine prices for your Subaru R2

problems.group: Timing Belt Failure on EN07 Engines
problems.h4: Timing Belt Failure on EN07 Engines - Interference Engine Risk
problems.affectedModels: Subaru R2 all variants (2003-2010, all EN07 codes)
problems.rootCause: EN07 is an interference engine - if the timing belt snaps, pistons collide with open valves causing catastrophic damage requiring full engine replacement.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace timing belt, tensioner, idlers and water pump at 60k mile interval
repairOptions.longevity: Essential preventative maintenance, avoids catastrophic failure

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt EN07 with new timing components, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Only option after belt failure causes valve/piston damage

problems.recommendation: Never skip the 60,000 mile timing belt service on your R2. If the belt has snapped, a rebuilt EN07 engine is the only viable repair - our specialists include new timing kits as standard.
problems.cta: Get quotes for Subaru R2 timing belt service or engine replacement

problems.group: Supercharger Failure on EN07X STi Engines
problems.h4: Supercharger Failure on EN07X STi Engines
problems.rootCause: IHI supercharger bearing wear causes boost pressure loss, whining noise and reduced performance; oil starvation accelerates failure on tracked or hard-driven examples.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove, disassemble and rebuild IHI supercharger with new bearings and seals
repairOptions.longevity: Restores boost if housing is undamaged, but labour-intensive

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt EN07X with new supercharger, intercooler hoses, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best value for STi owners, includes full performance restoration

problems.recommendation: On a valued R2 STi, a rebuilt EN07X engine with new supercharger offers better long-term reliability than a standalone supercharger rebuild. Our performance specialists include boost calibration as standard.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt EN07X STi engine prices for your Subaru R2

problems.h4: Crank Angle Sensor Failure - R2 Stalling Issue
problems.rootCause: Faulty crank angle sensor causes intermittent stalling, no-start conditions and engine management lights; subject to Subaru recall campaign on early R2 imports.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace crank angle sensor, clear fault codes, road test
repairOptions.longevity: Quick fix if sensor is sole issue, but verify no secondary damage

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt EN07 if sensor failure caused secondary damage
repairOptions.longevity: Only needed if stalling led to catalytic converter or engine damage

problems.recommendation: Most crank sensor failures are resolved with sensor replacement alone. However, if stalling caused catalytic converter damage or engine wear, a rebuilt EN07 engine may be the more economical long-term solution.
problems.cta: Get quotes for Subaru R2 crank sensor or engine replacement

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Subaru R2
finalCta.paragraph: The R2's compact EN07 engines are reliable when maintained, but oil consumption, timing belt risks and supercharger wear can lead to costly repairs. EngineMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK R2 specialists who understand JDM kei cars. Every rebuilt engine includes upgraded components to address known weaknesses, plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty and nationwide delivery.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Subaru R2 Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Subaru R2 and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineYears.h2: Subaru R2 Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This section covers all major engine changes across the R2's production timeline, organised so owners can quickly identify their engine and compare rebuilt engine prices from UK specialists.

years.description: First production year of Subaru R2 with EN07 engine family introduction for UK grey imports.
keyChanges: Subaru R2 launched in Japan December 2003, first UK grey imports arrive 2004
EN07E (46ps SOHC) and EN07D (54ps DOHC) engines introduced
R, S and Classic trim levels available with 2WD/4WD and manual/iCVT options
mainEngines: EN07E - 0.7L petrol SOHC naturally aspirated (R, Classic)
EN07D - 0.7L petrol DOHC AVCS naturally aspirated (S, Classic Premium)
knownFor: Early production crank angle sensor recall affecting 2003-2005 models
Timing belt service interval critical due to interference engine design
Oil consumption begins to appear on higher-mileage examples

keyChanges: November 2005 facelift introduces revised front/rear styling and interior updates
EN07X supercharged engine (64ps) launched exclusively for R2 STi performance variant
Improved emissions compliance and reliability refinements across EN07 range
mainEngines: EN07E - 0.7L petrol SOHC naturally aspirated (R, Classic)
EN07D - 0.7L petrol DOHC AVCS naturally aspirated (S, Classic Premium)
knownFor: EN07X supercharger bearing wear becomes common failure point on STi models
Facelift models benefit from updated crank sensor design reducing stalling issues
Final production year 2010 marks end of Subaru-built R2 before Daihatsu takeover

faq.h2: Subaru R2 Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does a Subaru R2 engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: Subaru R2 engine replacement costs typically range from £895 for a used EN07E to £3,250 for a rebuilt EN07X STi engine, depending on engine code, condition and warranty coverage.
keyPoints: Used EN07 engines start from £495-£1,250 supply only
Reconditioned EN07E/EN07D units range £895-£2,150 with 12-month warranty
EN07X supercharged STi engines command premium pricing £1,495-£3,250
Supply & fit labour adds £495-£895 depending on R2 drivetrain configuration
items.cta: Compare Subaru R2 engine prices from UK specialists

items.question: What engine codes fit the Subaru R2?
items.answer: The Subaru R2 exclusively uses EN07 series inline-4 engines: EN07E (46ps SOHC), EN07D (54ps DOHC), EN07X (64ps supercharged STi) and EN07V (46ps variant).
keyPoints: EN07E is the most common base engine for R and Classic trims
EN07D features DOHC and AVCS for S and Classic Premium variants
EN07X is exclusive to R2 STi with IHI supercharger and intercooler
Verify your R2's EN07 code via registration lookup before ordering replacement
items.cta: Check your Subaru R2 engine code compatibility now

items.question: Can I fit a Subaru Impreza or Legacy engine in my R2?
items.answer: No - Subaru R2 uses the unique EN07 inline-4 engine, not Subaru's boxer architecture. Impreza/Legacy boxer engines are physically incompatible with R2 chassis and mounting points.
keyPoints: R2 EN07 is a longitudinal inline-4, not Subaru's signature flat boxer design
Engine mounts, ECU, wiring harness and drivetrain are R2-specific
Attempting boxer engine swaps requires extensive custom fabrication
Stick with EN07 series replacements for reliable, plug-and-play compatibility
items.cta: Get quotes for genuine EN07 Subaru R2 engine replacements

items.question: How long does a reconditioned Subaru R2 engine last?
items.answer: A properly reconditioned EN07 engine with upgraded components can last 60,000-100,000+ miles with regular maintenance, matching or exceeding original engine lifespan.
keyPoints: Rebuilt EN07 engines include upgraded piston rings to prevent oil consumption
New timing belt kits and water pumps address common failure points
12-month unlimited mileage warranty provides peace of mind post-installation
Regular oil changes and timing belt services critical for longevity
items.cta: Find reconditioned Subaru R2 engines with warranty coverage

items.question: Does a Subaru R2 engine replacement include warranty?
items.answer: Yes - all engines supplied through EngineMarket include a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty, with extended 24-month options available for performance EN07X STi engines.
keyPoints: Standard 12-month warranty covers parts and labour for manufacturing defects
Extended warranties available for high-value R2 STi or collector examples
Warranty terms vary by supplier - confirm coverage details before ordering
Keep service records to maintain warranty validity post-installation
items.cta: View Subaru R2 engine warranty terms and options

items.question: How long does it take to replace a Subaru R2 engine?
items.answer: Engine supply typically takes 1-3 working days for UK stock EN07 units; professional fitting requires 1-2 days at a specialist garage familiar with R2 chassis and EN07 layout.
keyPoints: JDM import EN07 engines may require 7-14 days for shipping and customs clearance
R2's compact engine bay requires specialist knowledge for efficient removal/installation
ECU adaptation and immobiliser matching may add time for imported replacement engines
Book supply & fit service in advance to minimise vehicle downtime
items.cta: Book Subaru R2 engine supply & fit with UK specialists

items.question: What's the difference between rebuilt, reconditioned and used R2 engines?
items.answer: Used EN07 engines are second-hand units with minimal refurbishment; reconditioned engines are fully stripped and rebuilt with new wear items; rebuilt engines include performance upgrades and extended testing.
keyPoints: Used: Budget option, 30-90 day warranty, mileage and condition vary
Reconditioned: Balanced value, 12-month warranty, upgraded rings and seals
Rebuilt/Remanufactured: Premium option, extended warranty, dyno-tested performance
Choose based on your R2's value, intended use and budget constraints
items.cta: Compare Subaru R2 engine types and pricing options

items.question: Can I drive my Subaru R2 with a faulty engine to your fitting centre?
items.answer: We strongly advise against driving an R2 with major engine faults - oil consumption, timing belt issues or supercharger failure can cause catastrophic damage en route. Arrange recovery or collection.
keyPoints: Timing belt failure while driving will destroy an interference EN07 engine
Low oil pressure from consumption issues risks bearing damage and seizure
Supercharger failure on STi models can send metal debris through the engine
Use our recovery partner network or arrange transport for damaged R2s
items.cta: Arrange Subaru R2 engine recovery and replacement service

items.question: Is it worth replacing the engine in a Subaru R2?
items.answer: For well-maintained R2s valued over £1,500, engine replacement is often economical versus vehicle replacement - especially with budget EN07 options starting under £900.
keyPoints: R2 kei cars offer low running costs, insurance and tax post-repair
Rebuilt EN07 engines with warranty provide long-term reliability
Consider vehicle condition, mileage and sentimental value in decision
Get multiple quotes to compare repair vs replacement vehicle costs
items.cta: Get instant Subaru R2 engine replacement quotes to decide

items.question: What causes Subaru R2 engines to fail prematurely?
items.answer: The most common EN07 failure causes are oil consumption from piston ring wear, timing belt neglect on interference engines, and supercharger bearing failure on STi models.
keyPoints: Oil consumption leads to low oil levels, accelerated wear and potential seizure
Missed timing belt services cause catastrophic valve/piston damage
Supercharger oil starvation on tracked/hard-driven STi examples accelerates bearing wear
Regular maintenance and using rebuilt engines with upgraded components prevents recurrence
items.cta: Compare Subaru R2 engine solutions addressing common failure causes
